plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet the 20% single-name cap.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLV to diversify into healthcare, a strong scanner setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation and lower volatility.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for approximately 1.5% monthly growth, focusing on capital preservation. Expected drawdowns are lower due to reduced leverage and diversified holdings. However, growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% single-name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all leveraged ETFs and reduce overall risk.
Trim QQQI as flagged by the scanner and reallocate capital.
Add to CRWD, a strong performer in an uptrend, and buy ZS and GOOGL.
This plan balances growth with risk management, focusing on quality tech.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% monthly growth, emphasizing risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expected volatility is moderate, with controlled drawdowns.

moves.note: Allocate to top scanner setup, high R

plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet the 25% single-name limit.
Sell SOXL and SPAL, as they are below stop and flagged for exit.
Trim QQQI as flagged by the scanner to free up capital.
Increase CRWD exposure, as it is a strong uptrending name.
Buy ZS and NET, focusing on high-scoring scanner setups with good R-factors.
This plan aggressively leans into growth, using a smaller cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% monthly growth, aiming for stretched growth. It involves higher concentration in strong setups and a smaller cash buffer. Be aware of significantly higher potential drawdowns and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs if added back.

moves.note: Reduce broad index, shift to income
Increase income ETF, core of strategy
Reduce broad index, shift to income

plan: Trim SPY and QQQ to reduce broad index exposure and reallocate.
Significantly increase QQQI to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Sell CRWD and SMH to exit high-beta single names and sector exposure.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield with low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
income.read: This income plan aims for approximately 1% monthly distributions, prioritizing yield and low drawdown. Growth potential is minimal, and NAV can still fall in a market selloff. Distributions are variable and not guaranteed.
